ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general milk tea entry (FOODON_03301099); no specific Earl Grey milk tea variant with reduced sugar exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers milk tea broadly; no Earl Grey-specific or reduced-sugar variant code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Earl Grey milk tea with reduced sugar. SNOMED CT does not enumerate beverage preparation variants at this granularity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of Earl Grey milk tea (less sugar, no topping) is approximately 250 ml, equivalent to 250 g, providing about 103 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Earl Grey Milk Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Earl Grey milk tea (less sugar, no topping), CR ID D08010021. All per-100g values are taken as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — milk tea and sweetened tea beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Earl Grey milk tea (less sugar) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened milk tea with reduced sugar, which is anchored by the GI of lactose (~46) and diluted sugar in a milk-buffered beverage matrix. Carbohydrate per serving = 4.86 g/100g × 250 g serving = 12.15 g. GL = (45 × 12.15) / 100 = 5.5 (rounded). This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar with zero dietary fibre and low potassium in a sweetened milk tea beverage
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low at 12 mg/100g, the presence of added sugar (4.86 g/100g), zero dietary fibre, and negligible potassium in this sweetened milk tea makes it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension; only a genuinely unsweetened (0 g sugar) version would be appropriate.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (4.86 g/100g) with zero dietary fibre classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: Even at a reduced sugar level, this milk tea contains 4.86 g sugar per 100 g with no dietary fibre, qualifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA guidelines; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid it and choose plain unsweetened Earl Grey tea instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Fat content (2.41 g/100g) from milk or creamer with unknown saturated fat fraction and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The fat content of 2.41 g/100g from milk or creamer carries an unquantified saturated fat load, and the added sugar (4.86 g/100g) with zero fibre contributes to cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake and prefer unsweetened, low-fat tea alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar with zero fibre and no meaningful anti-inflammatory components
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage contains added sugar and zero dietary fibre with no omega-3 fatty acids or polyphenol-rich unsweetened tea base, failing to meet positive anti-inflammatory criteria; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should switch to unsweetened Earl Grey tea to access bergamot polyphenols without the inflammatory burden of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Earl Grey Milk Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping) is a popular Singaporean takeaway beverage consisting of black tea flavoured with bergamot oil, blended with milk or non-dairy creamer, and prepared with a reduced amount of added sugar and no customisable toppings such as pearls or jelly. The drink has a mild, floral-citrus aroma from bergamot, a creamy mouthfeel from the milk or creamer component, and a lightly sweet flavour profile. Per 100 g, it provides 41 kcal, 0.32 g protein, 2.41 g fat, 4.86 g carbohydrate (all as sugar), 0 g dietary fibre, and 12 mg sodium, as reported by the Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage still contains 4.86 g sugar per 100 g with zero dietary fibre, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage under clinical guidelines. It is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension due to the added sugar and absence of fibre and potassium. The fat content originates from milk or creamer and carries an unquantified saturated fat fraction. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low) based on analogous sweetened milk tea data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of approximately 5.5 per standard 250 ml serving. Unsweetened Earl Grey tea is the recommended alternative for those seeking the bergamot polyphenol benefit without the metabolic risk of added sugar.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (CR ID D08010021) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. All core macros are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence medium for GI component only); overall record confidence is high.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Earl Grey milk tea (less sugar, no topping) preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010021 or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent product as of 2026. 2. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; this is consistent with a milk-tea beverage containing no plant fibre components. 3. GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened milk tea with reduced sugar from the University of Sydney GI Database; direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available and is recommended. 4. The input dietary_tags included 'keto', which is not supported: with 4.86 g carbohydrate per 100 g and 12.15 g per standard 250 ml serving, this beverage does not meet strict ketogenic thresholds and the keto tag has been removed. 5. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for Earl Grey milk tea (less sugar) as a distinct food item.
